headline: Retired civil servants hold 340 GLC positions worth RM120 million
context: A governance audit found 340 retired senior civil servants occupying GLC board and advisory positions with combined annual compensation of RM120 million.

cards.big: 340 retired civil servants sit on GLC boards — earning RM120 million collectively
cards.sub: Revolving door between public service and government-linked companies

cards.big: No cooling-off period exists for civil servants joining GLCs they previously regulated
cards.sub: Officials can move from regulator to regulated entity on the same Monday morning

cards.big: Average GLC board compensation for retirees is RM353,000 per year on top of pension
cards.sub: Double-dipping through pension plus board fees costs public coffers significantly

cards.big: Singapore mandates 2-year cooling-off period for senior public officers joining GLCs
cards.sub: Cooling-off periods prevent regulatory capture and conflicts of interest

cards.big: When regulators retire into the companies they regulated, who was really in charge all along?
Should Malaysia mandate a 3-year cooling-off before retired civil servants join GLCs?
